entries.title: Factor Theorem
entries.content: A polynomial f(x) has a factor (x - c) if and only if f(c) = 0.
entries.example: Checking if (x-2) is a factor of x^2 - 4

entries.title: Factorial
entries.content: The product of all positive integers less than or equal to a given non-negative integer n.

entries.title: Factoring Polynomials
entries.content: Breaking down a polynomial into a product of simpler polynomials.
entries.example: Factoring a quadratic to find its roots

entries.title: Fallacy in Logic
entries.content: An error in reasoning that renders an argument invalid or unsound.
entries.formula: P => Q, Q implies P (Converse error)
entries.example: Assuming because A came before B, A caused B

entries.title: False Positive Rate
entries.content: In statistics, the probability of falsely rejecting the null hypothesis (type I error rate).
entries.example: Falsely flagging a clean email as spam

entries.title: F-Distribution
entries.content: A continuous probability distribution used frequently in ANOVA and tests of equality of variances.
entries.example: Comparing variances of two samples

entries.title: Feasible Region
entries.content: The set of all possible points of an optimization problem that satisfy its constraints.
entries.example: Shaded area in linear programming graph

entries.title: Fermat's Last Theorem
entries.content: No three positive integers a, b, and c satisfy the equation a^n + b^n = c^n for any integer value of n greater than 2.
entries.formula: a^n + b^n = c^n has no solution for n > 2
entries.example: Proven by Andrew Wiles in 1994

entries.title: Fermat's Little Theorem
entries.content: If p is a prime number, then for any integer a, the number a^p - a is an integer multiple of p.

entries.title: Fibonacci Sequence
entries.content: A sequence where each number is the sum of the two preceding ones, starting from 0 and 1.

entries.title: Field Definition
entries.content: An algebraic structure with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division satisfying field axioms.
entries.formula: Real numbers R form a field
entries.example: Rational numbers Q form a field under standard ops

entries.title: Finite Set
entries.content: A set that has a finite number of elements, which can be counted.
entries.example: The set of vowels in English: {a, e, i, o, u}

entries.title: First Derivative Test
entries.content: Using the first derivative to determine local extrema of a function by looking at sign changes.
entries.formula: f'(x) changes sign from + to - at local max
entries.example: Finding peak of y = -x^2 + 4x

entries.title: First-Order Logic
entries.content: Formal logic extending propositional logic by allowing quantifiers, predicates, and relations.
entries.example: Writing mathematical statements formally

entries.title: Fixed Point Theorem
entries.content: A theorem stating that a function will have at least one point mapped to itself under certain conditions.
entries.example: Brouwer fixed-point theorem

entries.title: Floor Function
entries.content: A function that takes a real number input and outputs the greatest integer less than or equal to it.

entries.title: Focus of Ellipse
entries.content: One of the two fixed points that define an ellipse; the sum of distances from any point on the ellipse to the foci is constant.
entries.example: Focal points of planetary orbit

entries.title: Focus of Parabola
entries.content: A fixed point used to define a parabola; every point on the parabola is equidistant from the focus and directrix.
entries.formula: Distance to focus = distance to directrix
entries.example: Focus of y = x^2 is at (0, 1/4)

entries.title: FOIL Method
entries.content: A mnemonic for multiplying two binomials: First, Outer, Inner, Last.
entries.formula: (a + b)(c + d) = ac + ad + bc + bd

entries.title: Fourier Series
entries.content: A way to represent a periodic function as the sum of simple sine and cosine waves.
entries.example: Decomposing square wave sound into harmonics

entries.title: Fourier Transform
entries.content: An integral transform that decomposes a function of time into its constituent frequencies.
entries.example: Converting signal from time domain to frequency domain

entries.title: Fractal Dimension
entries.content: A ratio providing a statistical index of complexity, describing how detail in a fractal pattern changes with scale.
entries.example: Sierpinski triangle has fractal dimension approx 1.585

entries.title: Fraction Simplification
entries.content: Reducing a fraction to its lowest terms by dividing the numerator and denominator by their greatest common divisor.
entries.example: Simplifying 6/8 to 3/4

entries.title: Fredholm Integral Equation
entries.content: An integral equation in which the limits of integration are fixed.
entries.formula: f(x) = g(x) + lambda * integral from a to b of K(x, t)*f(t) dt
entries.example: Solving electrostatic potential problems

entries.title: Frequency Distribution
entries.content: A table or graph displaying the frequency of various outcomes in a sample.
entries.formula: Count of occurrences of each data class
entries.example: Tally of grade counts A, B, C in a class

entries.title: Frequency Polygon
entries.content: A graph made by joining the midpoints of the tops of the bars of a histogram.
entries.formula: Points plotted at class marks with frequencies
entries.example: Visualizing distribution of test marks

entries.title: Frobenius Norm
entries.content: A matrix norm defined as the square root of the sum of the absolute squares of its elements.
entries.example: Calculating magnitude of a matrix

entries.title: Frustum Volume
entries.content: The volume of the portion of a cone or pyramid that remains after its top is cut off by a plane parallel to the base.
entries.example: Volume of a drinking bucket

entries.title: Function Composition
entries.content: Applying one function to the results of another.
entries.example: f(x) = sqrt(x), g(x) = x - 2 -> f(g(x)) = sqrt(x-2)

entries.title: Function Domain
entries.content: The set of inputs for which a function produces defined outputs.
entries.example: Domain of y = sqrt(x) is x >= 0

entries.title: Function Inverse
entries.content: A function that reverses the action of the original function.
entries.example: Inverse of f(x) = 2x is f^-1(x) = x/2

entries.title: Function Mapping
entries.content: A relation that associates each element of a set with a unique element of another set.
entries.example: Mapping student IDs to their grades

entries.title: Fundamental Homomorphism Theorem
entries.content: A theorem stating that the image of a homomorphism is isomorphic to the quotient by the kernel.
entries.example: Isomorphism between groups in abstract algebra

entries.title: Fundamental Theorem of Algebra
entries.content: Every non-constant single-variable polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root.
entries.formula: deg(p) = n => p(x) has n complex roots
entries.example: A cubic equation has exactly three roots (including multiplicity)

entries.title: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic
entries.content: Every integer greater than 1 either is a prime number itself or can be represented as the product of prime numbers uniquely.

entries.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
entries.content: Links differentiation with integration.
entries.formula: integral from a to b of f(x) dx = F(b) - F(a)
entries.example: Integrating 3x^2 to find area

entries.title: Fuzzy Logic
entries.content: A form of many-valued logic in which the truth values of variables may be any real number between 0 and 1.
entries.example: Defining temperature as 'warm' (0.7 true)

entries.title: Fuzzy Set
entries.content: A set whose elements have degrees of membership between 0 and 1.
entries.example: The set of 'tall people'

entries.title: Future Value formula
entries.content: The value of a current asset at a specified date in the future based on an assumed rate of growth.
entries.example: Investing $1000 today for 5 years at 5% interest

entries.title: F-Test statistics
entries.content: A test whose test statistic has an F-distribution under the null hypothesis, used to compare variances.
entries.example: Testing if two production lines have different variance
